component.name: OpenEmux
component.summary: Browse and play your retro game library

description.p: OpenEmux is a clean, GNOME-native frontend that makes RetroArch simple and enjoyable: add, browse and organize your retro game library by console and, with a compatible gamepad, play the games you already own. It was inspired by OpenEmu, bringing that same polished experience to Linux.

OpenEmux runs games through RetroArch, so the RetroArch Flatpak must be installed and its cores downloaded (via RetroArch's own Online Updater). Install it with: flatpak install flathub org.libretro.RetroArch
OpenEmux ships no games, ROMs or BIOS files. Use only content you legally own. Emulation matters for preserving the history and culture of video games — please respect the people who created them.

ul.li: A cover-art library organized by console, with favorites
Per-console keyboard and gamepad mappings
Per-console video shaders
BIOS status checks and cover-art syncing

screenshot.caption: The cover-art library for a console

description.p: Cartridge artwork, and managing ROMs from the library.

ul.li: Every game is shown inside its console's cartridge, composed from vector artwork and cached; nine consoles are covered and the look is on by default
The favorite star on a cover is a button now, so favoriting is one click
Rename a ROM from its context menu: the file, its artwork and the playlists all follow
Delete a ROM from its context menu; the file goes to the system trash, where it can be restored
Select several ROMs by dragging across the library, then delete them or sync covers for just those
The sidebar context menu stays open instead of closing as soon as the mouse button is released
Context menu entries now show an icon
A three-dot button on ROM covers and sidebar consoles opens the context menu with a plain click
The favorite star moved to the top-left corner of the cover

description.p: ROM import, archive support and controller mapping.

ul.li: ROMs inside .zip archives are recognized; archives are extracted for cores that need a real file
Import ROMs from the main window, by button or drag and drop
Map controls by pressing the buttons on the controller, with up to four gamepad ports
Optional ScreenScraper cover source, including cartridge label art
Rotating tips status bar and a rebindable fullscreen toggle
Cover sync can be stopped while it runs
Per-console cover proportions and a separate cartridge-label image slot
Redesigned "All" library view with per-game console labels
Background check for new releases on startup
Native .deb and .rpm packages alongside the AppImage
Cached cartridge textures and smaller frame assets
Documentation and website updates
Plays games through the RetroArch Flatpak
Renamed to OpenEmux for naming consistency
GNOME HIG interface built on libadwaita
Cover-art library, per-console input and shaders, BIOS checks
